strengths: 15+ years of professional full-stack experience with direct TypeScript, Node.js, and React expertise matching the primary stack (Optilyz, RIDE Capital, re:cinq)
Current AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq — MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — directly satisfies the AI agent development requirement
Deep testing culture: TDD practitioner across multiple roles, authored Fluent-Asserts library and Trial test runner, ran public TDD/Selenium workshops
concerns: NestJS is entirely absent from the resume despite being the specified primary backend framework
Vitest and Jest are not mentioned; testing tool experience is concentrated in custom D-lang frameworks and general TDD practice
Kotlin is not listed; Java experience is limited to robotic-arm work at Altom (2016–2018), making the secondary stack a weak point
response.justification: The candidate is a strong fit on the core primary stack — TypeScript, Node.js, and React are evidenced across three consecutive senior roles, and the current re:cinq position directly covers the AI agent development requirement via MCP tooling and agentic frameworks. Experience exceeds the 6-year threshold by a wide margin (15+ years professional). Cloud and containerization are covered (AWS CDK at Optilyz, Docker, Kubernetes in GISCollective). The main gap is NestJS, which is the prescribed backend framework and does not appear anywhere in the resume; given the candidate's Node.js depth this is learnable but unverified. Similarly, Vitest and Jest are not cited explicitly, and Kotlin is absent. These gaps prevent a top score but do not undermine the interview case given the candidate's demonstrated breadth and tooling-level testing expertise.
